title_en: Chapter 1: Mentioning the noble ancestry of the Prophet ﷺ
title_ar: الفصل الأول: ذكر نسب النبي الشريف ﷺ
title_id: Bab 1: Menyebutkan Nasab Mulia Nabi ﷺ
content_id:
Allah Ta'ala berfirman: 'Allah lebih mengetahui di mana Dia menempatkan risalah-Nya' [Surah al-An'am (6): 124]. Dan ketika Heraklius, raja Romawi, bertanya kepada Abu Sufyan tentang sifat-sifat Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, beliau berkata: 'Bagaimana status keluarganya di antara kalian?' Abu Sufyan menjawab: 'Beliau berasal dari keluarga yang baik (mulia) di antara kami.' Heraklius berkata: 'Sesungguhnya semua Rasul berasal dari keluarga mulia di antara kaum mereka masing-masing.' Beliau adalah pemimpin anak Adam dan kebanggaan mereka, di dunia dan di akhirat. Nama-nama dan gelar-gelarnya: Abul Qasim, Abu Ibrahim, Muhammad, Ahmad, al-Mahi (Penghapus) yang melaluinya Allah menghapus kufur, al-Aqib (yang terakhir datang) setelah beliau tidak ada Nabi lagi, al-Hasyir (pengumpul) yang di kakinya (yakni di belakangnya) manusia akan dikumpulkan, al-Muqaffi (yang terakhir dalam urutan), Nabi Rahmat, Nabi Taubat, Nabi al-Malhamah (pertempuran sengit), penutup (terakhir) para Nabi, dan Abdullah (hamba Allah).

Beliau adalah Muhammad ibn Abdillah ibn Abdil Muttalib ibn Hasyim ibn Abd Manaf ibn Qusayy ibn Kilab ibn Murrah ibn Ka'b ibn Lu'ayy ibn Ghalib ibn Fihr ibn Malik ibn Nadhr ibn Kinanah ibn Khuzaimah ibn Mudrikah ibn Ilyas ibn Mudhar ibn Nizar ibn Ma'd ibn Adnan, yang merupakan keturunan Ismail 'alaihis salam. Tidak perlu menyebutkan berbagai pendapat yang dimiliki para ulama mengenai berapa jumlah ayah di antara mereka (yakni antara Adnan dan Ismail 'alaihis salam). Tidak ada perbedaan pendapat di antara para ulama tentang nasab ini. Semua suku Arab di wilayah Hijaz menelusuri nenek moyang mereka hingga nasab ini. Itulah mengapa mengenai firman Allah Ta'ala: 'Katakanlah (wahai Muhammad): Aku tidak meminta kepada kalian atas ini suatu imbalan pun kecuali kecintaan kepada keluargaku.' [Surah al-Syura (42): 23] Ibn Abbas radhiyallahu 'anhuma dan lainnya berkata: 'Tidak ada satu pun keluarga (yakni sub-suku) Quraisy kecuali Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam memiliki kerabat di dalamnya, sehingga ayat di atas diturunkan berkenaan dengan ini, dan penafsirannya adalah: Wahai Quraisy! Aku tidak menginginkan apa pun dari kalian kecuali kalian berlaku baik kepadaku karena kekerabatanku dengan kalian.'

Telah diriwayatkan dari sanad yang bersambung dan terputus bahwa Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda: 'Aku lahir dalam pernikahan yang sah dan bukan dari perbuatan zina — dari (generasi pertama) anak-anak Adam hingga kedua orang tuaku melahirkanku, dan tidak ada sedikit pun perzinaan zaman jahiliyyah yang pernah menyentuhku.' Diriwayatkan secara autentik dalam Shahih al-Bukhari bahwa Abu Hurairah radhiyallahu 'anhu meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda: 'Aku telah diutus (sebagai Rasul) dalam generasi terbaik dari keturunan Adam sejak penciptaan mereka.' Dan dalam Shahih Muslim dari hadis Wathilah ibn al-Asqa' radhiyallahu 'anhu bahwa Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda: 'Sesungguhnya Allah telah memilih Ismail dari anak-anak Ibrahim, dan Dia memilih Banu Kinanah dari anak-anak Ismail, dan Dia memilih Quraisy dari Banu Kinanah, dan Dia memilih Banu Hasyim dari Quraisy, dan Dia memilih aku dari Banu Hasyim.'

Imam Ahmad meriwayatkan dari hadis al-Muttalib ibn Abi Wada'ah bahwa al-Abbas radhiyallahu 'anhu datang kepada Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, dan seolah-olah beliau shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam mendengar sesuatu yang dikatakan orang-orang. Maka Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam berdiri di atas mimbar dan berkata: 'Siapa aku?' Mereka berkata: 'Engkau adalah Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam.' Beliau shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda: 'Aku adalah Muhammad bin Abdillah bin Abdil Muttalib. Sesungguhnya Allah menciptakan makhluk dan Dia menempatkanku dalam golongan terbaik dari mereka, kemudian Dia menjadikan mereka dalam dua kelompok, maka Dia menempatkanku dalam kelompok terbaik dari mereka, kemudian Dia menjadikan mereka dalam suku-suku, maka Dia menempatkanku dalam suku terbaik di antara mereka, kemudian Dia menjadikan mereka dalam keluarga-keluarga, maka Dia menempatkanku dalam keluarga terbaik di antara mereka dari segi suku dan nasab.' Shalawat Allah dan salam-Nya atas beliau selamanya hingga Hari Kiamat. Dalam Shahih Muslim diriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda: 'Aku adalah pemimpin anak Adam pada Hari Kebangkitan, dan itu bukan kesombongan.'
content_en:
Allaah تعاىل said: ﴿ اللَّـه أ ع ل م ح ي ث َي ع ل ر س ال ت ه﴾ {Allaah knows best with whom to place His Message} [Surah al-An`aam (6): 124] And when Heraclius, the king of the Roman, asked Abu Sufyaan about the attributes of the Prophet ﷺ, he said: “What is his family status amongst you?” He (Abu Sufyaan) replied: “He belongs to a good (noble) family amongst us.” He said: “In fact, all the Messengers come from noble families amongst their respective peoples.” He is the leader of the children of Aadam and their pride, in this world and the Hereafter. His names and titles: Abu al-Qaasim, Abu Ibraaheem, Muhammad, Ahmad, al-Maahee (Obliterator) through whom Allaah obliterates Kufr (disbelief), al-`Aaqib (the last to come) after whom there will be no Prophet, al-Haashir (the gatherer) at whose feet (i.e. behind whom) mankind will be gathered, al-Muqaffee (the last in succession), the Prophet of Mercy, the Prophet of repentance, the Prophet of al-Malhamah (fierce battle), the seal (last) of the Prophets, and `Abdullaah (servant of Allaah). 1 Al-Baihaqee said: Some of the scholars added saying: Allaah تعاىل referred to him in the Qur’aan as: “Messenger, Prophet, unlettered, witness, bringer of good tidings, warner, the one who invites to Allaah – by His permission, illuminating lamp, kind and merciful, the one who reminds. And Allaah تعاىل made him as (source of) mercy, blessings, and guidance.” 1 Shaikh al-Albaanee said: The last two names and Ahmad, these three names are mentioned in the Qur’aan; the others are mentioned in the different Ahaadeeth. See `Aqeedah al-Tahaawiyyah (pg. 292) of Shaikh al-Albaanee’s checking; and al-Saheehah (1571, 1628); and al-Rawdhatul-Nadheer (401, 1017). [TN: See also sahih al-Bukhaaree (3532, 4896) and sahih Muslim (2354, 2355), and Musnad Ahmad (19525, 19621, 19651, 23443, 23445)]

He is Muhammad Ibn `Abdullaah Ibn `Abdul-Muttalib Ibn Haashim Ibn `Abd Munaaf Ibn Qusayy Ibn Kilaab Ibn Murrah Ibn Ka`b Ibn Lu’ai Ibn Ghaalib Ibn Fahr Ibn Maalik Ibn Nadhr Ibn Kinaanah Ibn Khuzaimah Ibn Mudrikah Ibn Ilyaas Ibn Mudhar Ibn Nizaar Ibn Ma`d Ibn `Adnaan 2 and who is from the sons of Ismaa`eel. There is no need to mention the different opinions (the scholars) have regarding how many fathers are there between them (i.e. `Adnaan and Ismaa`eel عليه السالم). 3 There is no difference of opinion amongst the scholars about this lineage. 4 All the `Arab tribes in al-Hijaaz area trace back their ancestry to this lineage. This is why regarding the saying of Allaah تعاىل: ﴿ق ل َّّل أ س أ ل ك م ع ل ي ه أ ج رًا َّإ ّل ال م و دَّة ف ال ق ر ب ى﴾ {Say (O Muhammad): “No reward do I ask of you for this (message) except to be kind to me for my kinship with you.”} [Surah al-Shoora (42): 23] Ibn `Abbaas رضي اهلل عنهما and others said: ❝There was not a single house (i.e. sub-tribe) of Quraish but the Prophet ﷺ had relatives therein and so the above Aayah was revealed in this connection, and its interpretation is: “O Quraish! I do not want 2 sahih al-Bukhaaree (5/44) without a chain 3 [TN: Imaam Ibn Katheer mentioned in his “al-Seerah al-Nabawiyyah”: The scholars differed in the numbers of fathers between Ismaa`eel and `Adnaan. The highest number that was said is forty, and the minimum is four. This is why Imaam Maalik رمحه اهلل disliked attributing the lineage to the Prophet ﷺ after `Adnaan. `Urwah Ibn al-Zubair (the son of Asmaa' Bint Abu Bakr and the nephew of `Aaishah رضي اهلل عنهم أمجعني) said: “We have not found anyone who is sure (with certainty) about those between `Adnaan and Ismaa`eel.”] 4 Shaikh al-Albaanee said: This is the reason why I have mentioned it in this “sahih al-Seerah”, adhering to the principles that I have clarified and mentioned earlier in the introduction. And Abu Zahrah, the author of “خامت النبيني”, as evidence, relied on the narration of Ibn `Abbaas رضي اهلل عنهما that whenever Prophet ﷺ spoke of his ancestry he would stop at `Adnaan and say: ❝Genealogists tell lies.❞ Allaah تعاىل says: ﴿و قـ ر ونًا بـ ني ذ ىل ك ك ث ريًا﴾ {and many generations in between} [Surah al-Furqaan (25): 38] And it was hidden from him that this is a fabricated narration. In its chain is a liar who himself confessed that he used to lie. See al-Dha`eefah (111)]

anything from (you) except that you should be kind to me for my kinship with you.”❞ It has reported from the connected and disconnected chains that the Prophet ﷺ said: ❝I was born in marriage and not out of wedlock - from the (first) children of Aadam till my parents bore me, 6 and nothing from the fornication of the days of ignorance ever touched me.❞ 7 It is authentically reported in sahih al-Bukhaaree that Abu Hurairah رضي اهلل عنه narrated that Allaah’s Messenger ﷺ said: ❝I have been sent (as a Messenger) in the best of all the generations of Aadam’s offspring since their Creation.❞ 8 And in sahih Muslim from the hadith of Waathilah Ibn al-Asqa` رضي اهلل عنه that the Messenger of Allaah ﷺ said: ❝[Indeed Allaah has chosen Ismaa`eel from the children of Ibraaheem], and He chose Banu Kinaanah from the children of Ismaa`eel, and He chose the Quraish from Banu Kinaanah, and He chose Banu Haashim from Quraish, and He chose me from Banu Haashim.❞ 9 Imaam Ahmad reported from the hadith of al-Muttalib Ibn Abee Wadaa`ah that alAbbaas رضي اهلل عنه came to the Messenger of Allaah ﷺ, and it is as if he ﷺ heard something which the people were saying. So the Prophet ﷺ stood upon the Minbar (pulpit) and said: ❝Who am I?❞ They said: “You are the Messenger of Allaah ﷺ.” He ﷺ said: ❝I am Muhammad bin `Abdullaah bin `Abdul-Muttalib, indeed Allaah created the creation, and He put me in the best [group] of them, then He made them into two groups, so He put me in the best group of them, then He made them into tribes, so He put me in the best of them in tribe, then He made them into houses, so 5 sahih al-Bukhaaree (3497, 4818) 6 Meaning: his ﷺ lineage all came down to him through lawful marriage and never through fornication. 7 Shaikh al-Albaanee said: I have discussed the various chain of this narration in al-Irwaa (1914) and with its various chains, the hadith reaches to the level of “Hasan”. And for this reason, I have mentioned it in sahih al-Jaami` alSagheer (3223, 3224, 3225) 8 sahih al-Bukhaaree (3557); see also al-Saheehah (809). 9 sahih Muslim (2276) and the first part is not from sahih Muslim; it from Sunan al-Tirmidhee (3605). See alSaheehah (302)

He put me in the best of them in tribe and lineage.❞ 10 صلوات اهلل وسالمه عليه دائما أبدا إىل يوم الدين In the sahih (of Imaam Muslim), it is reported that the Messenger of Allaah ﷺ said: ❝I am the leader of the children of Aadam on the Day of Resurrection, [and it is no boast].❞ 11 10 Musnad Ahmad (1788) and Sunan al-Tirmidhee (3532, 3608) [TN: Shaikh al-Albaanee had first authenticated this hadith in sahih al-Jaami` (1472), but later he retracted and graded it as “Dha`eef” in al-Dha`eefah (3073)] 11 sahih Muslim (2278) [TN: The wording in the bracket are from Sunan al-Tirmidhee (3148, 3615) and Sunan Ibn Maajah (4308) and graded as “sahih” by Shaikh al-Albaanee]
content_ar:
قال الله تعالى: ﴿اللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ حَيْثُ يَجْعَلُ رِسَالَتَهُ﴾ [سورة الأنعام (6): 124]. ولمّا سأل هرقلُ ملكُ الروم أبا سفيان عن صفات النبي ﷺ، قال له: «ما حسبه فيكم؟» فأجابه أبو سفيان: «هو فينا ذو حسب.» فقال هرقل: «وكذلك الرسلُ تُبعث في أحساب قومها.» وهو ﷺ سيد ولد آدم وفخرهم في الدنيا والآخرة. أما أسماؤه وألقابه فهي: أبو القاسم، وأبو إبراهيم، ومحمد، وأحمد، والماحي الذي يمحو الله به الكفر، والعاقب الذي ليس بعده نبيّ، والحاشر الذي يُحشر الناس على قدمه، والمقفّي، ونبي الرحمة، ونبي التوبة، ونبي الملحمة، وخاتم الأنبياء، وعبد الله.

قال البيهقي: وزاد بعض العلماء، قائلين: إن الله تعالى وصفه في القرآن الكريم بـ«الرسول والنبي والأمي والشاهد والمبشر والنذير والداعي إلى الله بإذنه والسراج المنير والرؤوف الرحيم والمذكّر، وجعله الله رحمةً ونعمةً وهادياً.» وقال الشيخ الألباني: أما آخر اسمين وأحمد فهذه الثلاثة مذكورة في القرآن، وسائرها مذكور في الأحاديث المختلفة. انظر: عقيدة الطحاوية (ص 292) بتخريج الشيخ الألباني، والسلسلة الصحيحة (1571، 1628)، والروضة الندية (401، 1017). [وانظر أيضاً صحيح البخاري (3532، 4896) وصحيح مسلم (2354، 2355) ومسند أحمد (19525، 19621، 19651، 23443، 23445)].

وهو محمد بن عبد الله بن عبد المطلب بن هاشم بن عبد مناف بن قُصيّ بن كلاب بن مُرَّة بن كعب بن لؤي بن غالب بن فهر بن مالك بن النضر بن كنانة بن خزيمة بن مدركة بن إلياس بن مضر بن نزار بن معد بن عدنان، وهو من أبناء إسماعيل عليه السلام. ولا حاجة إلى ذكر الخلاف في عدد الآباء بين عدنان وإسماعيل عليه السلام. وهذا النسب مجمع عليه بين العلماء، وإليه يرجع جميع قبائل العرب في الحجاز. ولهذا قال ابن عباس رضي الله عنهما وغيره في قوله تعالى: ﴿قُل لَّا أَسْأَلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ أَجْرًا إِلَّا الْمَوَدَّةَ فِي الْقُرْبَىٰ﴾ [سورة الشورى (42): 23]: «لم يكن بطن من بطون قريش إلا وللنبي ﷺ فيه قرابة، فنزلت هذه الآية في هذا المعنى»، أي: يا معشر قريش، لا أطلب منكم أجراً على تبليغ الرسالة إلا أن تودّوني لقرابتي منكم.

ومن أعلى روايات هذا النسب ما رواه البخاري في صحيحه من حديث ابن عباس رضي الله عنهما مرفوعاً: «إن الله اصطفى من ولد إبراهيم إسماعيل، واصطفى من ولد إسماعيل بني كنانة، واصطفى من بني كنانة قريشاً، واصطفى من قريش بني هاشم، واصطفى مني بني هاشم، واصطفاني من بني هاشم.» فهو ﷺ خلاصة الاصطفاء من آدم إلى خاتم الأنبياء.
